title: Log File Investigation
description: Use `grep`, `head`, and `tail` to analyze a sample access log.
problemStatement:
You are analyzing a web server log (`access.log`) to identify potential security issues.

**Setup:** Create a sample log file with the following command:
```bash
echo -e '192.168.1.1 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:55:36] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 2326
192.168.1.100 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:56:01] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 404 209
192.168.1.1 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:56:20] "GET /login.php HTTP/1.1" 200 445
192.168.1.200 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:57:10] "GET /wp-admin HTTP/1.1" 404 209
192.168.1.1 - - [10/Oct/2023:13:58:00] "POST /login.php HTTP/1.1" 401 521' > access.log
```

**Tasks:**
1. View the first 3 lines of the file using `head`.
2. View the last 2 lines of the file using `tail`.
3. Find all lines containing `404` (Page Not Found errors).
4. Find all lines that start with the IP address `192.168.1.1`. (Hint: Use `^`).
5. Count how many times `login.php` was accessed. (Use `grep -c` or pipe to `wc -l`).
solutionCode:
head -n 3 access.log
tail -n 2 access.log
grep '404' access.log
grep '^192.168.1.1' access.log
grep -c 'login.php' access.log
